projects.name: ASR-Lab
projects.description: A centralized engineering workbench for cross-project visibility and automated laboratory logs.

projects.name: Sahayak-AI-Powered-Assistive-Navigation-System
projects.description: Core project mission and technical overview... Sahayak (meaning "Helper" in Hindi) is a next-generation mobility and navigation assistant designed exclusively for the visually impaired. Relying securely on real-time computer vision and voice commands, Sahayak acts as a "virtual eye"—solving the very real danger of navigating unpredictable urban Indian environments. By fusing Edge Computing (AI) with High-Precision Maps, it promotes true independence.
contributions: Engineered a Voice-First interface using Web Speech API for 100% hands-free
Integrated TensorFlow.js (COCO-SSD) to perform offline, real-time object detection
projects.outcome: Won the "Best Innovation Award" by successfully delivering a working, low-latency prototype of a "virtual eye" navigation assistant. The application empowers visually impaired users to navigate urban environments independently and safely by executing real-time AI computer vision directly on their mobile edge devices without relying on slow server roundtrips.

projects.name: SugarSense
projects.description: SugarSense is a full-stack diabetes management platform designed to help users track health data and access personalized insights.
contributions: Implemented authentication to protect user-specific records
Integrated external APIs using documentation-driven approach
Supported end-to-end deployment and live demo readiness
projects.outcome: Enabled secure, user-specific diabetes data management through authenticated APIs, delivered as a working prototype under hackathon constraints.

9f2c2376-6d2d-5ff3-bf3e-b15fb706863b.title: Shifted AI Inference to the Edge (Browser)

details.description:
Navigating urban environments requires split-second hazard alerts. Relying on a backend API for computer vision would introduce dangerous latency (network roundtrips). We decided to run the coco-ssd model directly on the user's device using TensorFlow.js in the browser.

> Outcome: Achieved near-zero latency for object detection and auditory warnings, keeping users safe offline or on poor network connections.

derived.summary_text:

details.description:
Successfully delivered a working prototype of a voice-guided, complete assistive navigation system for the visually impaired. Integrated TensorFlow.js for in-browser, offline hazard detection and MapmyIndia for exact hyper-local routing

> Outcome: Proved that low-latency AI inference at the mobile edge is a viable, life-saving solution for real-time pedestrian navigation, resulting in the Best Innovation Award.

notes.title: Dhwani.ai
notes.content:
## Banking That Listens to Bharat

Dhwani.ai is an AI-powered voice banking assistant that speaks Indian languages — Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, and English. No menus. No typing. Just speak.

---

## The Problem

- **700+ million Indians** prefer voice over typing
- Rural bank customers struggle with English-only interfaces
- Call centers can't scale to handle regional languages
- Elderly users find app navigation frustrating

---

## The Solution

A voice-first banking assistant where users speak naturally in their language and receive instant voice responses. Say "Mujhe loan chahiye" in Hindi or "எனக்கு கடன் வேண்டும்" in Tamil — Dhwani understands.

---

## How It Works

```
🎤 User speaks
    ↓
🔊 Sarvam STT transcribes (saarika:v2.5)
    ↓
🔒 PII auto-redacted (Aadhaar, phone, email)
    ↓
🧠 Groq AI processes (Llama 3.3 70B)
    ↓
🗣️ Sarvam TTS responds (bulbul:v3)
    ↓
🎧 User hears natural voice response
```

---

## What Makes It Special

| Feature | Why It Matters |
|---------|----------------|
| **6 Indian Languages** | Real multilingual — Hindi, Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, Marathi, English |
| **Sub-4 second responses** | Groq's speed makes it feel like talking to a human |
| **Privacy-first** | Aadhaar, phone numbers auto-masked before AI sees them |
| **Compliance-ready** | All conversations logged, sensitive queries flagged for human review |
| **Voice-only UX** | No typing needed — accessibility for all literacy levels |

---

## Tech Stack

- **Voice AI**: Sarvam AI — India's best speech models
- **LLM**: Groq AI — Llama 3.3 70B at insane inference speed
- **Backend**: Python 3.11+, FastAPI, SQLite
- **Security**: PII redaction, audit trails, human review flagging

---

## Use Cases

1. **Bank Call Centers** — Automate customer queries with AI that speaks their language
2. **Mobile Banking** — Voice-enabled banking for users who prefer speaking over typing
3. **Branch Kiosks** — Self-service voice assistants at bank branches
4. **Rural Outreach** — Banking access for users with limited literacy

---

## Sample Interactions

| Language | User Says | AI Understands |
|----------|-----------|----------------|
| Hindi | "Mujhe home loan chahiye" | Home loan inquiry |
| Tamil | "எனக்கு கடன் வேண்டும்" | Loan information request |
| Telugu | "నాకు బ్యాలెన్స్ చెక్ చేయాలి" | Balance check |
| Bengali | "আমার অ্যাকাউন্ট খুলতে চাই" | Account opening |
| Marathi | "मला FD rates हवे आहेत" | FD rate inquiry |

---

## Performance Specs

| Parameter | Value | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| STT Timeout | 8 sec | Prevents hanging on bad audio |
| AI Timeout | 3.5 sec | Fast fallback to human agent |
| TTS Speed | 1.25x | Natural, brisk conversation |
| Max Tokens | 400 | Concise, relevant responses |

---

## Links

- **GitHub**: https://github.com/Ashurai84/Dhwani.ai


---

## Built

March 9, 2026 — Rapid prototype demonstrating voice-first banking UX for Indian languages. One evening build.

notes.title: KARMIC
notes.content:
## 1. Vision

Karmic is a text‑based spiritual clarity chatbot where users express their thoughts freely and receive guidance in the voice of Shree Krishna. The goal is not motivation or therapy, but clarity, responsibility, and right action.

---

## 2. Why Karmic Exists

### Problem

- People share worries but feel judged or misunderstood.
- Friends and social media give opinions, not clarity.
- Prayer gives emotional release but no direct response.
- Many repeat the same mistakes because they lack reflection and responsibility.

### Core Insight

People do not always need comfort. They need clear guidance without judgement.

---

## 3. Founder Intent (Why Us)

Karmic is built to help people process confusion, guilt, fear, and relationship dilemmas through wisdom-based reflection rather than instant advice. The product aims to transform emotional chaos into responsible action.

---

## 4. One-Line Definition

Karmic lets users speak freely and receive responsibility-focused guidance expressed as Shree Krishna, grounded in the Bhagavad Gita.

---

## 5. What Karmic Is NOT

- Not a therapy app
- Not a motivation app
- Not astrology or prediction
- Not a replacement for faith or religion
- Not a generic AI chatbot

---

## 6. Core Philosophy (Non-Negotiables)

1. Karma over emotion.
2. Clarity over comfort.
3. Guidance over dependency.
4. Judgement may exist, humiliation never.
5. User remains responsible for decisions.

---

## 7. V1 Scope (MVP)

### Included

- Text chat only
- Conversation history
- Single persona: Krishna
- Response format:
    - Thought
    - Explanation
    - Reflection

### Excluded (V1)

- Voice input or voice replies
- Daily prompts
- Multiple personas (no Ram mode)
- Community features
- Notifications

---

## 8. User Experience Flow

1. User opens Karmic.
2. User writes freely (no forced prompt).
3. System analyzes intent and emotion.
4. Krishna responds:
    - Thought (mirror user’s inner state)
    - Explanation (clear reasoning based on karma/dharma)
    - Reflection (responsibility-oriented next direction)
5. Conversation is saved in history.

---

## 9. Krishna Response System

### Tone

- Familiar but authoritative.
- Calm, direct, and grounded.
- Hinglish for normal conversation.
- Sanskrit + Hindi only for shlok references.

### Tone Restrictions

Krishna never uses:

- Apology language (e.g., “sorry if…”)
- Casual slang (bro, dude, lol, etc.)

---

## 10. Shlok Usage Logic

Shlok appears only when relevant — not in every response.

Trigger rule:

- At least 2 conditions must be true:
    - Dharma conflict
    - Moral/ethical tension (paap context)
    - Emotional suffering (dukh)
